---
title: "LRPGF01: Gain factors for word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF01 is a **gain-factors** model for word reading (`ewrswr`): what is associated
with how much children *gain* across the three period transitions
(t1 $\rightarrow$ t2, t2 $\rightarrow$ t3, t3 $\rightarrow$ t4), modelled as an
ANCOVA — the period post-count given its pre-count — on the logit scale with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood and a child random intercept.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Under the locked study DAG (`notes/202606231600-dag-revision-consolidated.md`),
**only the randomised on-intervention term is causal** — its coefficient is
identified by the period-1 randomised contrast and reproduces the ITT effect.
Every other coefficient (own baseline, age, cognitive ability, the upstream skills
L and R, and all interactions) is an **adjusted association** confounded by latent
general ability (GA); the child random intercept repairs time-invariant GA up to
shrinkage, so same-wave predictors are "go-together" associations. Report these as
"associated with", **never** "drives". Intervention sessions / dose are never
conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded: it is not a DAG node and was
found statistically redundant.

:::

**DAG factor set:** on-intervention (causal) · own baseline · age · cognitive
ability (block design) · letter sounds (L) · receptive vocabulary (R) · child
random intercept. **Focal interactions:** group $\times$ ability,
group $\times$ own-baseline, age $\times$ ability.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,p} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}[p]}
  + \beta_{\text{trt}}\,\text{OnIntervention}_{i,p}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \sum_{s\in\{L,R\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \textstyle\sum\text{interactions}
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

with $W_{\text{post}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$,
$N = 79$, and $u_{\text{child}} \sim \operatorname{Normal}(0, \sigma_{\text{child}})$.
`OnIntervention` is the immediate arm from period 1 and the waitlist from period 2
(post-crossover). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
